DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-05-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Daniel Dressman, Esq. 415-875-4303 of Gordon Rees for Defendant BOARD OF TRUSTEES OF CALIFORNIA STATE UNIVERSITY.
No other appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. Defendant's Counsel reports the matter is not contested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BOARD OF TRUSTEES OF CALIFORNIA STATE UNIVERSITY's DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendant the Board of Trustees of California State University's demurrer to plaintiff Paul Sherwin's first amended complaint is sustained with 5 days leave to amend. Plaintiff's operative pleading still fails to state facts sufficient to show satisfaction of the statutory requirement for claim presentation. It also fails to state facts sufficient to support waiver or estoppel. The Court grants Plaintiff leave to amend, to allege, if he can do so in good faith, that per Gov. Code 915 (e)(3) his claim was actually received by the Trustees of the California State University or he was excused from doing so. (See DiCampli-Mintz v. County of Santa Clara (2012) 55 Cal.4th 983, 991). The Court, guided by California's liberal policy for allowing amendment to pleadings and exercising its inherent power per Le Francois v. Goel (2005) 35 Cal.4th 1094 also reconsiders and vacates the portion of its order from October 1, 2018, sustaining the cause of action for breach of contract without leave to amend. Plaintiff is given one final opportunity to amend his pleading and state facts, if he can do so in good faith, supporting a viable breach of contract cause of action based on a valid contract that contains a specific term or condition not in conflict with statutory terms and conditions of employment. Plaintiff's counsel must carefully review the case law in this area to determine if such facts can be stated and is directed to review those cases including Guarino v. County of Siskiyou (2018) 21 Cal.App.5th 1170. (See also Retired Employees Assn. of Orange County, Inc. v. County of Orange (2011) 52 Cal.4th 1171, 1182-1183; Shaw v. Regents of University of California (1997) 58 Cal.App.4th 44, 55). The Court understands the position of the Defendant but it must also strike a balance between Defendant's legitimate legal arguments and the liberal policy in our state to allow amendments to pleadings in the early stage of litigation. That being said, this is the final opportunity for Plaintiff to either allege in good faith facts to allow him to proceed on his contract claim or to understand that no such facts exist. Plaintiff must make both amendments (on claim presentation and his contract cause of action) within 5 days from December 5, 2018.

Defendant's Counsel submits a proposed order.

Judge: Anne Christine Massullo, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/ACM)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-12-2018 Continued To Dec-26-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-12-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Andrew Pierce, Esq., 650. 843.1900, appearing for the Plaintiff;
Daniel H. Dressman, Esq. of Gordon & Rees, 415.875.4303, appearing for the Defendant Board of Trustees Of California State University.

DEFENDANT BOARD OF TRUSTEES OF CALIFORNIA STATE UNIVERSITY DEMURRER to COMPLAINT

Matter called, ARGUMENT held.

At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling:

Defendant San Francisco State University's demurrer as to the entire complaint is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. The law is well settled that a complaint is subject to general demurrer where a plaintiff fails to allege timely compliance with the presentation requirement set forth in Government Code 905, 910 and 915 or allege facts excusing compliance. (State of California v. Superior Court (2004) 32 Cal.4th 1234, 1240-1241.) Plaintiff fails to provide a declaration in support of his opposition providing any evidence that he can meet the administrative exhaustion requirements. Nonetheless, in his opposition papers he alleges, through counsel, that he is able to meet his pleading burden. He will have 10 days from the date of the hearing, September 12, 2018, to file a First Amended Complaint. Defendant San Francisco State University's demurrer as to the First and Second Causes of Action is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. Public employees cannot state causes of action for breach of contract or breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing in the employment context. (See Shoemaker v. Myers (1990) 52 Cal. 3d 1, 23-24 citing Miller v. State of California (1977) 18 Cal. 3d 808, 813 ("[I]t is well settled in California that public employment is not held by contract but by statute and that, insofar as the duration of employment is concerned, no employee has a vested contractual right to continue in employment beyond the time or contrary to the terms and conditions fixed by law").) Plaintiff offers to legal authority to support his contract causes of action or refute the clear weight of legal authority to the contrary. Counsel for the Defendant is to prepare a form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Anne-Christine Massullo, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Rebecca Tovar, CSR 14200, 415.551.3778, Email: RTovar@sftc.org. =(302/ACM)
